title: Deep authority-boundary field renames

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Expand PR #1244 to include deep authority-boundary renames for reportable AW advisory/mechanical fields that overstate semantic authority.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Do not rename legitimate host decision-record/ADR fields or true hard-gate command names. Preserve compatibility only as explicit deprecated aliases when required.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Exact canonical field names, alias strategy, and test updates for startup/implement/report surfaces.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A rename would break package-owned Planning/Memory contracts, remove hard-gate clarity, or require broad unrelated architecture changes.
canonical_core.next_action: Close this item after committing the completed deep authority-boundary renames.
proof_expectations: focused startup/implement/report tests covering renamed authority fields
contract, inventory, maintainer, schema-reference, and generated-package checks
completion_criteria: Canonical reportable AW fields no longer use decision/classification/route_candidate where the value is advisory or mechanical.
Deprecated compatibility aliases, if kept, are explicitly named as legacy aliases and excluded from new docs/tests.
Tests assert new canonical names on startup/implement/report surfaces.
Proof passes and PR #1244 remains scoped to close #1243.

goal: Create a bounded plan for Deep authority-boundary field renames.
non_goals: Leave adjacent backlog or follow-on work out of this plan.

intent.outcome: Deep-rename reportable AW advisory/mechanical authority fields for #1243.
intent.constraints: Do not rename true host decision records, ADR fields, or hard-gate command names.
intent.latitude: Use canonical field additions plus legacy aliases where compatibility requires.
intent.escalation: Escalate before package-owned breaking changes or broad unrelated architecture changes.

execution.next_step: Close the planning item and update PR #1244.
execution.proof: Focused renamed-field tests, make test-workspace, make lint-workspace, and generated command package check passed.

invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Complete #1243 including deep naming cleanup so AW reportable surfaces do not imply AW owns advisory semantic classification or routing.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Compact AW reportable packets now teach canonical advisory/mechanical names instead of authority-overstating legacy names.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: make test-workspace, make lint-workspace, generated command package check, focused start/implement/proof tests, and dogfooding outputs passed.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Expand the scope to include deep renaming.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Rename canonical public/reportable AW advisory fields, not only add explanatory authority_boundary metadata.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Add canonical alternatives for planning safety result, delegation recommendation, changed-path facts, and candidate route; update compact projections/tests/docs while keeping explicit legacy aliases only where needed.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm the scaffolded plan still matches the promoted item before broad implementation.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: src/agentic_workspace/workspace_runtime_primitives.py; tests/test_workspace_start_preflight_cli.py; tests/test_workspace_implement_cli.py; tests/test_workspace_report_cli.py; .agentic-workspace/docs/reporting-contract.md; generated references if schema/docs require; this execplan and planning state/archive.
execution_bounds.max changed files: About 10 plus generated references if needed.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: focused renamed-field tests; make test-workspace; make lint-workspace; contract tooling; structured inventory; maintainer-surfaces; schema-reference-docs; generated package check; planning surfaces; summary; doctor.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before removing compatibility aliases or changing package-owned Planning/Memory schemas.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated ADR/decision-record support, #1241 decision-traceability, or package internals not required by generated refresh.

stop_conditions.stop when: The work no longer matches the promoted item or its completion criteria.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A correct fix requires changing the requested outcome or ownership boundary.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Implementation needs files outside the filled execution bounds.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The selected proof cannot demonstrate the completion criteria.

context_budget.live working set: This execplan, the promoted item, and the narrow files needed for the current implementation step.
context_budget.recoverable later: Repo background, historical reviews, and deferred backlog unless compact outputs point there.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, proof_report, finished_run_review, and closeout_distillation before pausing.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Deep rename AW advisory/mechanical public fields: planning_safety_gate result, delegation recommendation, changed path facts, candidate route.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Expand #1243/#1244 to include deep canonical field renames.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Keep true hard gates clear; do not rename unrelated host decision-record concepts.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Canonical field names and legacy alias strategy.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: Compatibility requires removing fields consumed outside workspace tests.

post_decomposition_delegation.decision rule: After this slice is bounded, decide whether direct work, read-only exploration, implementation handoff, validation handoff, or stronger review improves quality or saves tokens safely.
post_decomposition_delegation.required evidence: slice id, route, reason, quality risk, token-saving class, read-first refs, write scope, proof burden, stop conditions, and return contract

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Preserve the larger intended outcome separately from this bounded slice.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Keep the slice bounded while carrying any larger follow-on through explicit continuation fields.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Did this slice advance the declared larger outcome, or only complete the local task?

active_milestone.scope: Canonical renames for AW advisory/mechanical reportable fields.

immediate_next_action: Implement canonical renamed fields and update tests/docs.
invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.
completion_criteria: Canonical fields use `gate_result`, `recommended_route`, `changed_path_facts`, and `candidate_route` for AW-owned gates, advisory recommendations, mechanical facts, and route candidates.
Legacy `decision`, `changed_path_classification`, and `route_candidate` fields are either removed from compact public surfaces or explicitly kept as deprecated aliases where compatibility requires.
Tests and docs assert the renamed canonical fields.

execution_run.what happened: Added canonical names for workflow sufficiency, planning safety, delegation recommendations, changed-path facts, route candidates, and proof route selection; updated compact projections, docs, and tests.
execution_run.scope touched: runtime projections, reporting contract docs, and CLI tests
execution_run.validations run: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_start_preflight_cli.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py::test_proof_supports_exact_field_selectors_for_sufficiency -q; focused implement/proof tests; make test-workspace; make lint-workspace; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py; ruff check; stale-reference rg sweep reviewed

delegation_outcome_feedback.route skipped reason: Kept local because the slice was already bounded and validation-heavy.

proof_report.validation proof: make test-workspace; make lint-workspace; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: Focused tests passed for start, implement, proof, and exact sufficiency selector; dogfooding showed compact outputs using sufficiency_result, recommended_route, gate_result, changed_path_facts, candidate_route, and proof_route_selection; stale-reference sweep found only compatibility aliases or non-target intent/ADR decision fields.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Create a bounded plan for Deep authority-boundary field renames.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: Canonical fields are present and asserted in compact projections/tests; legacy aliases are explicit compatibility aliases only.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Deep authority-boundary renaming completed for reportable advisory/mechanical surfaces.
execution_summary.resume from: archive; no continuation for this bounded #1243/#1244 rename slice

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open. Prefer `agentic-planning archive-plan <plan> --prepare-closeout` before hand-editing closeout fields.
closure_check.why this decision is honest: The scope requested was deep renaming for authority-overstating reportable fields; compact surfaces now use canonical names and proof passed.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Validation commands and dogfooding output recorded in proof_report and execution_run.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Reopen only if PR review finds a reportable advisory/mechanical compact surface still teaching the deprecated names as canonical.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, report AW smoothness/helpfulness gaps, better-way signals, unused-feature reflections, and places AW could help more. Route each concrete signal to exactly one owner class unless explicitly split, or mark no_signal_found after checking.
signals dismissed: Generated package refresh produced no checked-in diff because generated content was already content-equivalent.
